title: Nottingham & Hardwick Hall (via Tonge)

main_route.original_wording: Tonge [?H#*] near Nottingham, on the Cloud Cycleway
main_route.excerpt: We drove to __Tonge__ [?H#*] near Nottingham, on the Cloud Cycleway. Thou the gate we were on the cycleway. Warm day.
We drove to Nottingham + had lunch in a pub next to the Kanal. Good, Selaft! ... We walked up thru Gatehouse to the Castle museum.
Tea back at the van + we have driven to another field – Pleasleyfarm campsite * *. We went along a Roman road out of Nottingham – slow + bordered by buildings.
Sunday ... After breakfast on to Hardwick Hall.
Off 2:30 pm after a good lunch We looked round house + now going home.

excursions.label: Morning cycle along Cloud Cycleway to River Trent

stops.original_wording: Trent River, disused, often embanked railway line thru fields
stops.excerpt: We cycled 3 miles up to the Trent River, disused, often embanked railway line thru fields.

unresolved.issue: Illegible annotation after 'Tonge' on page B_0247_L; possibly a campsite name or grid reference; Tonge itself is identified as near Nottingham on the Cloud Cycleway (consistent with Tonge, Leicestershire)
unresolved.excerpt: We drove to __Tonge__ [?H#*] near Nottingham, on the Cloud Cycleway.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: page B_0248_L is marked boundary_mixed=true; trip ends mid-page after 'now going home'
summary: A three-day Easter weekend camping trip by Simon, Eve, and dog Tinder to the East Midlands, staying first near Tonge on the Cloud Cycleway and cycling to the River Trent, then visiting Nottingham Castle museum before moving to Pleasleyfarm campsite. The final morning was spent at Hardwick Hall, Derbyshire, before returning home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: Diary spans pages B_0247_L, B_0247_R, B_0248_L. Page B_0247_R carries printed page number 67. Page B_0248_L is boundary_mixed (trip ends mid-page). The sons are all mentioned contextually (scattered at their own homes/activities) but did not travel on this trip. The annotation '[?H#*]' after Tonge is illegible and flagged as unresolved. 'Kanal' in Nottingham likely refers to the Nottingham Canal area near the castle. 'Pleasleyfarm campsite' is near Pleasley, Derbyshire (close to the Nottinghamshire border, near Hardwick Hall). Hardwick Hall is a National Trust Elizabethan house; the diary notes Bess of Hardwick's granddaughter Arbella Stuart kept prisoner there.
